title: Invalidate cache on write deterministically
description: Writes to the source delete or update the affected cache keys so reads cannot serve indefinitely-stale data.
body: Use when the same data is both written to a source and cached. Do not rely on TTL alone to clear stale data after an update, which leaves a window of wrong reads as long as the TTL. Actions: on each write, delete (preferred over update, to avoid races) the precise cache keys derived from the changed entity; key naming must make the affected keys computable from the write. Validation signals include a test that an update is reflected on the next read rather than serving the old cached value until expiry. Risk boundary: invalidation that cannot enumerate all derived keys (e.g. list/aggregate caches) leaves stale data; flag the un-invalidatable keys and shorten their TTL or version the key namespace.
